title: pteridium aquilinum subsp. japonicum
intro: Pteridium aquilinum subsp. japonicum, also known as the Japanese bracken, is a subspecies of the fern Pteridium aquilinum, native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pteridium aquilinum subsp. japonicum include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and potentially treating various diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disorders. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to promote overall health and well-being.
